title: OREGON TALKING BOOK AND BRAILLE LIBRARY - 211info
text: STATE LIBRARY OF OREGON OREGON TALKING BOOK AND BRAILLE LIBRARY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides audio and braille books, magazines, voters' guide, instructional music materials, and musical scores that people can borrow. Offers audio book players to registered individuals to use on extended loans. Textbooks, academic journals, and listening music are not available. 250 Winter Street NE Salem, OR 973013950 Eligibility: Individuals with a print disability (a person who cannot read 12-point print for long periods of time comfortably). This includes legal blindness and other visual, physical, and reading impairments. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (503) 378-5389 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 378-5389 Toll-free (800) 452-0292 In Oregon Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information or to apply. Materials can be mailed or downloaded. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Completed application with Certification of Eligibility AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Assistive Technology Equipment Provision Options Braille Materials/Collections Home Library Services Reading Services for People with Disabilities Other Services Offered At This Location: INFORMATION SERVICES - STATE LIBRARY OF OREGON If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. STATE LIBRARY OF OREGON
